title: Content Security Policy (CSP) Quiz
description: Test your knowledge of the essential security headers and directives for protecting your web applications.

questions.question: What is the primary role of a 'Content Security Policy' (CSP)?
options: To make the website load much faster.
To detection and mitigate certain types of attacks, such as Cross-Site Scripting (XSS).
To define the website's visual theme and fonts.
To store sensitive user passwords on the server.

questions.question: Which of these is the MOST common directive used to set a default policy for all content sources?
options: script-src
default-src
style-src
img-src

questions.question: How can you specify a list of trusted domain names for JavaScript files in a CSP?
options: Use the 'trusted-script-src' directive.
Use the 'script-src' directive with a list of domains.
Use a 'meta rel' tag for each domain.
CSP does not allow specifying domain names.

questions.question: By default, a strict CSP will ________ all inline JavaScript scripts (like onclick attributes) for security.
options: Block / Disable
Allow / Execute
Ignore but warn
Automatically encrypt

questions.question: What is a 'Nonce' in the context of CSP?
options: A special font used for encryption.
A random, unique string that must match for a specific script to be allowed to run.
A type of CSS animation.
A browser extension used for debugging.
